chapter_title.ar: باب الْمُتَوَفَّى عَنْهَا زَوْجُهَا الْمَدْخُولِ بِهَا أَيْنَ تَعْتَدُّ وَ مَا يَجِبُ عَلَيْهَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 45 – The woman widowed from her husband who has been slept with, where should she wait from and what is Obligated upon her

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/200)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/200)
text: جَاءَتِ امْرَأَةٌ إِلَى أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) تَسْتَفْتِيهِ فِي الْمَبِيتِ فِي غَيْرِ بَيْتِهَا وَ قَدْ مَاتَ زَوْجُهَا فَقَالَ إِنَّ أَهْلَ الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ كَانَ إِذَا مَاتَ زَوْجُ الْمَرْأَةِ أَحَدَّتْ عَلَيْهِ امْرَأَتُهُ اثْنَيْ عَشَرَ شَهْراً فَلَمَّا بَعَثَ اللَّهُ مُحَمَّداً ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) رَحِمَ ضَعْفَهُنَّ فَجَعَلَ عِدَّتَهُنَّ أَرْبَعَةَ أَشْهُرٍ وَ عَشْراً وَ أَنْتُنَّ لَا تَصْبِرْنَ عَلَى هَذَا .

en.hubeali: Humeyd, from Ibn Sama’at, from Muhammad Bin Abu Hamza, from Abu Ayoub, from Muhammad Bin Muslim who said,

‘A woman who came over to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> to seek a verdict regarding the spending of a night in other than her own house, and her husband had died. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘In the Pre-Islamic period, when a husband of a woman died, his wife would mourn over him for twelve months. So when Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Sent Muhammad<sup>saww</sup>, was Merciful on their weak ones, so He<sup>azwj</sup> Made their waiting period being of four months and ten (days), and you all are not being patient upon this?’
en.sarwar: 8. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ali ibn al-Hakam from al-‘Ala’ ibn Razin from Muhammad ibn Muslim who has said the following: “I once asked one of the two Imam, (abu Ja‘far or abu ‘Abd Allah), ‘Alayhim al-Salam, about the case of a woman whose husband dies and about where she can complete her waiting period. He (the Imam) said, ‘She can do so wherever she likes but she must not pass a night in a place other than her house.’”
